heading: API Keys Overview
body:
> **Important:** We recommend you use service accounts instead of API (Application Programming Interface) keys to
> authenticate to the Atlas Administration API. API (Application Programming Interface) keys are a legacy
> authentication method.

API keys are a legacy method of authenticating to the
Atlas Administration API that uses [HTTP Digest Authentication](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digest_access_authentication).

API (Application Programming Interface) keys have two parts: a Public Key and a Private Key. These serve
the same function as a username and a password to authenticate API (Application Programming Interface)
requests. To learn how to construct an API (Application Programming Interface) request using API (Application Programming Interface) keys,
see [Make an API (Application Programming Interface) Request](example-api-request).

Atlas hashes the Public Key and Private Key using a unique value
called a **nonce**. The nonce is only valid for a short amount of time
as per the [HTTP Digest Authentication](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digest_access_authentication) specification. This limited lifespan protects
against replay attacks, where an attacker caches a Private Key to use it
without time restriction.

Each pair of API (Application Programming Interface) keys belongs to only one organization, and can grant
access to any number of projects in that organization. To give
organization-level API (Application Programming Interface) keys access to a project, see
[Grant Existing Service Account Access to a Project](invite-org-app-api-keys).

Atlas [Atlas User Roles](user-roles) limit which operations API (Application Programming Interface) keys
can perform. You must assign roles to API (Application Programming Interface) keys as you would for users
to ensure the API (Application Programming Interface) keys have the necessary permissions for the desired
API (Application Programming Interface) calls.

You can't use API (Application Programming Interface) keys to log into Atlas through the Atlas UI.
API (Application Programming Interface) keys only grant access to the Atlas Administration API, which does not
include UI access or access to cluster data.
